message:
fix: six security/correctness fixes across HTTP, HPACK, NATS, scheduler, transport

1. http/h1/codec.rs — Request smuggling: parse_chunk_size_line was
   trimming whitespace from chunk-size values, which could allow a
   lenient parser to interpret " 0A" differently from a strict proxy
   seeing "0A".  Fix: reject leading/trailing whitespace explicitly
   with BadChunkedEncoding error instead of trimming.

2. http/h2/hpack.rs — HPACK table overflow: DynamicTable::insert used
   unchecked addition (self.size + entry_size) which could wrap on
   extreme inputs.  Fix: use saturating_add.  Encoder::set_max_table_size
   now caps the size at MAX_ALLOWED_TABLE_SIZE before applying, preventing
   a peer from requesting an arbitrarily large dynamic table.

3. messaging/nats.rs — Subscription::next checked the closed flag before
   draining buffered messages, silently discarding messages dispatched
   between the last next() call and close().  Fix: try_recv any buffered
   messages before checking the closed flag.

4. runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs — EXP3 weight overflow: the adaptive
   cancel-streak policy's weight update could produce inf/NaN from
   repeated exponentiation.  Fix: clamp weights to [1e-30, 1e30] after
   each update, preserving relative ranking while keeping arithmetic
   stable.

5. transport/sink.rs — BufferedSink had no cap on staged_symbols when
   the inner sink was blocked, leading to unbounded memory growth.
   Fix: return Poll::Pending when staged queue exceeds 2x capacity
   (minimum 16), applying backpressure to the caller.

6. tests/fabric_replay_scenarios.rs — Fix borrow-after-move in
   schedule_leaf_partition: pass summary by &Arc reference instead of
   owned Arc; add explicit drop(bridge) before locking summary to
   prevent potential deadlock from lock ordering.  Adds
   clippy::too_many_lines suppression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
